title: fix: Change plan manager prompt in order to avoid calling tools directly
body:
Related to #2777
在默认plan manager prompt中加入了对tool直接调用的严格限制

# How Has This Been Tested?

经过对话测试，plan manager 成功避免直接调用 Tools

# Snapshots:

(images omitted)

step_plan.summary: Update the Planner/Plan Manager to prevent it from instructing the LLM to call tools directly by strengthening prompts and adding runtime safeguards. As defense-in-depth, inject a strict 'no tool calls' runtime hint and validate parsed plans to reject tool-invocation attempts.

steps.operation: Enforce 'no direct tool calls' in the planner profile at construction time
steps.reason: Ensure every PlannerAgent instance uses prompt text that explicitly forbids direct tool invocation. Programmatically injecting or overriding profile fields at __init__ centralizes the restriction (English and Chinese) so class-level constants or future dynamic selection can't omit it.
steps.side_effects: Changes the default prompt for all PlannerAgent instances, which may alter planner output and downstream behavior. Unit/integration tests that assert exact prompt text or plan wording will need updates. No API/schema changes, but planner behaviors in existing conversations may change; validate with conversational tests to ensure plans remain acceptable and parsed correctly.

steps.operation: Add an explicit runtime hint into the reply message context to prevent tools being invoked via rendered 'agents' content
steps.reason: The expand_prompt template renders reply_message.context['agents'] into the model prompt. Appending or adding a separate context key with a strict single-line instruction (e.g., 'Do NOT call tools or execute agents directly; only produce plan steps assigning agents to tasks.') ensures the rendered prompt always contains the restriction even if the profile text is modified or localized incorrectly.
steps.side_effects: Alters the content passed to the LLM, potentially changing model outputs. If templates that consume 'agents' assume a particular format, that rendering may need minor adjustments. Tests that examine full prompt text will need updating. This is prompt-level behavior only and does not change persisted data models or APIs.

steps.operation: Add post-parse validation to detect and reject tool-invocation attempts in plans
steps.reason: Prompt instructions are the primary control but can be bypassed. Adding validation after the LLM output is parsed into PlanInput items provides defense-in-depth: detect common calling patterns (e.g., 'run', 'execute', 'call tool', code-like function calls, or tool syntax) in item.content or forbidden directives in item.agent and either sanitize or reject the plan with a clear error message.
steps.side_effects: May cause previously accepted plans containing tool-call phrasing to fail validation; callers must handle validation errors. Tests for PlanAction.run must be extended to cover valid plans, rejected plans, and the exact error behavior. No external API schema changes, but user-facing tooling/UIs should surfacing validation failures so operators can re-prompt the planner.
